reasoning: Arizona is entering the 2026 NCAA tournament as a #1 seed after winning the Big 12 tournament on March 14, defeating Houston. Throughout February and early March, Arizona has been the betting favorite or co-favorite (with Michigan) to win the national championship, with sportsbooks pricing them around +425 to +450 right before the tournament. An outright odds price of +425 translates to approximately 19% implied probability. Removing typical sports betting vig, the true market probability is around 16-17%. While Polymarket had them at 12% on March 6, completing a conference tournament victory over a top-tier team like Houston likely increased market confidence and their chances. Historically, the overall pre-tournament favorite has about a 15-20% chance of winning the grueling 6-game single-elimination tournament. I am predicting 17%.
